notes: OWASP publishes multiple control catalogs and verification standards: ASVS (web app verification), MASVS (mobile app verification), and the AI Exchange (AI-system controls). These are normative requirements catalogs — what to implement and verify — distinct from OWASP's testing methodology guides (WSTG → secid:methodology/owasp.org/testing-guide).

data.description: Catalog of normative security requirements for designing, building, and testing web applications. Organized into chapters and sections (V1 Architecture, V2 Authentication, etc.) with per-requirement IDs. Defines three verification levels (L1 opportunistic, L2 standard, L3 advanced) to scope which requirements apply.
data.notes: ASVS is the de-facto reference for web app security verification — widely cited in compliance contexts (PCI DSS, SOC 2 control mapping) and procurement requirements. The companion MASVS covers mobile apps. ASVS 4.0.x ID format is V<chapter>.<section>.<requirement> (e.g., V1.1.1). ASVS 5.0 (in development as of 2025) restructures into chapter-level IDs.

examples.note: Architecture, Design and Threat Modelling — Secure SDLC requirement

match_nodes.description: OWASP Mobile Application Security Verification Standard

data.official_name: OWASP Mobile Application Security Verification Standard
data.description: Catalog of normative security requirements for mobile apps (iOS and Android). The mobile counterpart to ASVS. MASVS 2.x reorganized into category-prefixed IDs covering storage, crypto, authentication, network, platform, code quality, resilience, and privacy.
data.notes: Part of the OWASP Mobile Application Security (MAS) project, which also produces the MASTG (Mobile Application Security Testing Guide — a testing methodology) and MAS Checklist. MASVS 2.0+ uses MASVS-<CATEGORY>-<N> ID format (e.g., MASVS-AUTH-1). Older MASVS 1.x used MSTG-<CATEGORY>-<N> for testing controls (mostly phased out as MASVS separated requirements from testing).

urls.note: MAS Checklist — operational view of MASVS controls

children.description: MASVS 2.x category — a group of related controls
OWASP AI Exchange control ID — uppercase concatenated words
